author: gb9t
content:
Scope: reimplement grsec's GR-RBAC framework as a Rookery-original stackable LSM, plus reimplement gradm as a Rust userspace tool. Decided 2026-05-09 per user direction.

Source material:
- /home/doll/grsec-6.6.102.patch — the open 2018-era grsec patch contains the kernel-side RBAC engine (security/grsecurity/) and gradm userspace source (separately distributed but architecturally documented in the patch's docs/headers)
- .design/references/grsec-pax-notes.md — feature catalog + LSM-coexistence model

Architectural shape (from .design/security/00-overview.md):
- Tier-3 hub: .design/security/grbac/00-overview.md
- Spawned Tier-3 sub-docs (planned): policy-engine.md, gradm.md (userspace tool spec), domains.md, subjects.md, objects.md, inheritance.md, learning-mode.md, audit.md
- CONFIG_LSM_GRBAC=y in default Kconfig
- Built as a proper stackable LSM (uses LSM hooks; coexists with SELinux/AppArmor/etc.)
- Default policy empty/passive at boot (drop-in compat preserved)
- gradm tool: shipped as default userspace utility analogous to RHEL's selinux-utils / Debian's apparmor-utils
- GRKERNSEC_* feature flags become policy-loadable settings of GR-RBAC, NOT default-on system-wide
- License: GPL-2.0 (Rookery's GPL-clean reimplementation from the 2018 open patch; not claiming compat with current closed grsec)

Implementation gates:
- Block on issue #1 (Phase B done; this is Phase C/Tier-3 work)
- Block on issue #3 (Rookery clippy lints — hard prerequisite for any subsystem implementation per 00-rust-conventions.md D1)
- 00-security-principles.md (issue #2) should reference GR-RBAC policy file syntax once the Tier-3 docs land

Effort estimate: GR-RBAC kernel engine ~10K LoC reimplementation; gradm userspace ~few-K LoC. Comparable in scope to porting a medium LSM like SMACK or TOMOYO.

The user's direction: 'we can build gradm into the system by default the source code exists for that too we can reimplement it'. Implementation absorbs both kernel-side and userspace-side.
